=== Quantum Amplitude Estimation for Tail Risk Analysis ===

Risk Model Configuration:
  Loss distribution qubits: 4 (2^4 = 16 discrete levels)
  Loss threshold: 2.5
  Distribution: Log-normal(μ=0, σ=1)
  Theoretical tail probability P(Loss > 2.5): 0.16136312961328608

QAE Algorithm Parameters:
  Precision qubits: 6 (phase resolution: π/64)
  Repetitions: 120
  Total qubits: 11 (loss + precision + marker)

=== Canonical QAE Results (precision=6 bits, runs=120) ===
Phase measurement histogram (top 10):
  Phase 0/64 (θ=0, P≈0): 95 times
  Phase 32/64 (θ=1.5707963267948966, P≈1): 20 times
  Phase 24/64 (θ=1.1780972450961724, P≈0.8535533905932737): 2 times
  Phase 23/64 (θ=1.1290098598838318, P≈0.8171966420818227): 1 times
  Phase 40/64 (θ=1.9634954084936207, P≈0.8535533905932737): 1 times
  Phase 41/64 (θ=2.012582793705961, P≈0.817196642081823): 1 times
Most frequent outcome: phase=0/64, θ=0, P≈0
Mean amplitude estimate: 0.20162544546619554 ± 0.03598122817323795
Theoretical tail probability: 0.16136312961328608
Relative error: 24.95137268928775%

=== Classical Baseline Comparison ===
Monte Carlo (10000 samples): 0.16136312961328608 ± 0.003678655597017638
  Relative error vs theoretical: 0%

=== Quantum Advantage Analysis ===
QAE precision: ε ≈ 0.03598122817323795
MC precision: ε ≈ 0.003678655597017638
Precision improvement factor: 0x
Query complexity: QAE uses O(1/ε) = O(27.792269768706177) oracle calls
                  MC uses O(1/ε²) = O(10000) samples

=== Summary ===
Theoretical:     P = 0.16136312961328608
QAE estimate:    P = 0.20162544546619554 ± 0.03598122817323795
MC estimate:     P = 0.16136312961328608 ± 0.003678655597017638
QAE demonstrates quadratic speedup: O(1/ε) vs O(1/ε²) for precision ε
